title: Research & Development Costs
event: During the year, Hollis Co. incurs: laboratory research $120,000; prototype construction $45,000; and $60,000 for lab equipment with alternative future uses (10-year life, straight-line). Compute this year's R&D expense.

computationPaths.narration: GAAP expenses R&D as incurred — with one carve-out: equipment with ALTERNATIVE future uses is capitalized, and its depreciation flows into R&D expense.

memorize.body: R&D expense = all research and development costs as incurred + depreciation on capitalized alternative-use R&D equipment.
Two-way trap: expensing the alternative-use equipment (too much) or forgetting its depreciation belongs to R&D (too little).

questions.prompt: Research $120,000, prototype $45,000, alternative-use equipment $60,000 (10-yr SL). R&D EXPENSE this year = ?

distractors.feedback: You expensed the equipment outright. Alternative future uses → capitalize it; only its 6,000 depreciation hits R&D.
You capitalized the equipment but dropped its depreciation — that 6,000 belongs IN R&D expense: 171,000.
